question: Will Meta (META) close at $600-$610 on the final day of trading of the week of Mar 23 – Mar 27?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Meta (META) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Meta (META) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/META/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for Meta's closing price the week of March 23 hinges on macroeconomic uncertainty from impending CPI inflation data on March 26, pitting a 27.5% implied probability of sub-$560 against 25.5% for surpassing $650, with clusters around $590-$600 reflecting the recent $592 close. Bulls cite Meta's robust 28% ad revenue growth and Llama 4 AI model hype, positioning it competitively against OpenAI and Google in generative AI, while bears highlight $64 billion AI capex burn and looming EU regulatory fines. High volatility stems from broader tech sector dynamics, where ad platform dominance battles TikTok uncertainties, keeping odds tightly matched absent clear catalysts.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket gives Meta (META) roughly 55% implied odds of closing the week of March 23 above $580, propelled by robust momentum from its latest Q4 earnings beat and aggressive AI capex guidance exceeding $60 billion annually, underscoring Llama 4 model advancements amid intensifying competition from OpenAI and Google DeepMind. question: BNB Up or Down - March 22, 6PM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the close price is greater than or equal to the open price for the BNB/USDT 1 hour candle that begins on the time and date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Down".

The resolution source for this market is information from Binance, specifically the BNB/USDT pair (https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BNB_USDT). The close « C » and open « O » displayed at the top of the graph for the relevant "1H" candle will be used once the data for that candle is finalized.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BNB/USDT, not according to other exchanges or trading pairs.

question: Will Tesla (TSLA) close at <$360 on the final day of trading of the week of Mar 23 – Mar 27?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Tesla (TSLA) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Tesla (TSLA) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/TSLA/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket shows razor-thin implied probabilities evenly split at 49% across $360-$405 bins for Tesla's (TSLA) closing price the week of March 23, underscoring high uncertainty in a post-election rally now pushing shares near $345. Primary drivers include the 40% surge since November 5 on Musk-Trump policy tailwinds favoring autonomy deregulation and FSD acceleration, bolstered by Q3 earnings beats and Cybercab hype, yet tempered by 100x forward P/E valuations and softening EV demand signals from rivals like BYD. Key differentiators hinge on Q4 results (January 29) and Q1 deliveries (early April), with Fed rate cuts supporting growth but competition and China exposure creating downside risks below $370 versus breakout potential above $395 if robotaxi milestones materialize.

question: Will NVIDIA (NVDA) close at <$155 on the final day of trading of the week of Mar 23 – Mar 27?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for NVIDIA (NVDA) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket trader consensus clusters Netflix (NFLX) weekly close the week of March 23 tightly between $90-$100 (51% implied probability) and $100-$110 (49.5%), reflecting balanced sentiment amid streaming sector headwinds. Primary drivers include decelerating U.S. subscriber growth and escalating content spend, offset by Netflix's ad-tier revenue surge (up 34% YoY recently) and live sports pivot with WWE Raw rights, differentiating it from cash-burning rivals like Disney+ and Paramount+. Elevated interest rates cap multiple expansion for growth stocks like NFLX (forward P/E ~35x), with macro trader bets hinging on Q1 earnings previews; a break above $110 signals bullish international momentum, while sub-$90 flags recessionary ad pullbacks.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Netflix, Inc. (NFLX) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/NFLX/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket tilts toward a "Yes" for Netflix (NFLX) closing the week of March 23 above its key threshold, with implied probabilities around 55-60% reflecting optimism from robust subscriber growth and ad-tier revenue acceleration reported in Q4 2024 earnings. NFLX shares trade near $740 currently, up 15% YTD amid streaming market consolidation, but face headwinds from macroeconomic slowdown risks and competition from Disney and Amazon Prime. Key catalysts include the March 18-19 FOMC meeting, where Fed rate cut signals could boost tech valuations, and pre-Q1 earnings positioning ahead of late-April release; watch for $750 resistance level as resolution hinges on weekly close above the strike amid volatile Nasdaq dynamics.

question: Will Palantir (PLTR) close at <$146 on the final day of trading of the week of Mar 23 – Mar 27?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Palantir (PLTR) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Palantir (PLTR) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/PLTR/history, published under "Historical Prices."

eventMetadata.context_description: Polymarket traders see Palantir (PLTR) closing the week of March 23 in a razor-thin range around $152, with implied probabilities tightly clustered at 48% across $146-$164 buckets, signaling consensus on range-bound trading after a 300% YTD surge fueled by AI-driven commercial revenue growth topping 40% YoY in Q4. This even split reflects balanced bets amid overbought RSI levels near 80, valuation debates at 95x forward sales, and broader tech rotation risks from Fed repricing fewer 2025 rate cuts. Differentiating catalysts include prospective S&P 500 inclusion sparking passive inflows versus profit-taking on fading post-earnings momentum, with Nasdaq futures and PLTR volume as key resolution thresholds.

question: Will Opendoor (OPEN) close at <$1.00 on the final day of trading of the week of Mar 23 – Mar 27?
description:
This market will resolve according to the official closing price for Opendoor (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday).

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

If the final session of the week is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Yahoo Finance after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the official closing price for Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) on the final day of trading of the specified week (normally Friday) is higher than the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No."

If the final session is shortened (for example, due to a market-holiday schedule), the official closing price published for that shortened session will still be used for resolution.

If no official closing price is published for that session (for example, due to a trading halt into the close, system issue, delisting, or other disruption), the market will use the last valid on-exchange trade price of the regular session as the effective closing price.

The resolution source for this market is Yahoo Finance, specifically the Opendoor Technologies Inc. (OPEN) "Close" prices available at https://finance.yahoo.com/quote/OPEN/history, published under "Historical Prices."

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Yahoo Finance.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket for Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) closing above key thresholds the week of March 23 hinges primarily on persistent high mortgage rates curbing iBuying activity, with 30-year fixed rates hovering near 6.8% per Freddie Mac data, squeezing the company's revenue model amid low housing inventory. Recent Q4 results showed a narrowed net loss to $40 million but declining contribution profit per home, signaling ongoing margin pressure; shares trade around $1.80, down 70% YTD, reflecting trader consensus on real estate slowdown. Upcoming catalysts include March 19 FOMC dot plot updates on rate cuts—vital for OPEN's recovery—and weekly jobless claims data, with bulls eyeing a break above $2 resistance for bullish resolution odds.

question: Celtics vs. Grizzlies: O/U 232.5
description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 8:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Celtics and Grizzlies combine to score 233 or more points in this game.

If the combined total is less than 233, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

events.title: Celtics vs. Grizzlies
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for March 20 at 8:00PM ET:
If the Celtics win, the market will resolve to "Celtics".
If the Grizzlies win, the market will resolve to "Grizzlies".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: Grizzlies' mounting injuries dominate trader consensus, pricing Celtics at 82.5% implied probability for this matchup. Memphis faces a depleted roster with Ja Morant sidelined by hamstring strain, Desmond Bane out with a toe injury, and Jaren Jackson Jr. questionable per latest injury reports, gutting their scoring and defense against Boston's elite offense. Celtics enter healthy and surging on a four-game win streak, boasting top-tier efficiency led by Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown. Boston's home-court edge and superior recent form against sub-.500 teams further solidify the odds, reflecting historical dominance in similar lopsided scenarios despite basketball's upset potential.

question: Spread: FC Ōsaka (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for April 17 at 6:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Ōsaka" if FC Ōsaka win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Ehime FC".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on jleague.jp. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: FC Ōsaka vs. Ehime FC - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for April 17 at 6:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ehime FC enters as the clear favorite against J3 side FC Osaka in this J.League Cup matchup, with trader consensus reflecting their higher-division pedigree and recent momentum from two straight J2 wins, including a solid defensive showing. FC Osaka, struggling with just one victory in their last five J3 outings, faces roster challenges after midfielder Yuya Yamagishi's confirmed hamstring strain sidelines him for weeks, per official reports. Home advantage at Yodoko Sakura Stadium could spark an upset, but Ehime's superior head-to-head history (2-0 aggregate in prior cups) and rest edge post-midweek tilt odds further in their favor, underscoring crowd wisdom on potential scoreline shifts.

If the Sharks win, the market will resolve to "Sharks".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods and shootouts. In the event of a shootout, one goal will be added to the winning team's score for the purpose of resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Philadelphia Flyers enter as clear trader favorites against the San Jose Sharks, fueled by San Jose's league-worst 1-7-1 record and ongoing rebuild struggles, including a .878 team save percentage amid shaky goaltending from Vitek Vanecek and Georgiev. Recent Flyers momentum—three wins in four games, powered by Matvei Michkov's eight points and Travis Konecny's scoring surge—contrasts sharply with the Sharks' six-game skid. No major Flyers injuries per official reports, while San Jose misses defenseman Marc-Edouard Vlasic (upper body, week-to-week) and forward Ty Dellandrea (shoulder). Home-ice at Wells Fargo Center and Sharks' road woes (0-4-1 away) amplify the edge, though fatigue from San Jose's back-to-back looms.
